parts.answer: Know ledge 2 Up to 2 marks for defining the term ‘consumer income’ e.g. • The money earned/received (1) from work/investments (1). (2) Quest ion Number Using this data, calculate the total demand for Vietjet Air seats each day. You are advised to show your working. Answ er Mark

Know ledge 1, Application 2, Analysis 1 Quantitative skills assessed: QS1 Calculate, use and understand ratios, averages and fractions Know ledge 1 mark for identifying a suitable formula for likely demand: • Number of seats available per day x average percentage of seats sold (1) Applicat ion Up to 2 marks for selecting the correct data • 80 x 240 x 3 flights (1) x 88% (1) Analysis 1 mark for calculating the likely daily demand • 50,688 seats (1) NB: If no w ork ing is shown aw ard mark s as below : If answer given is 50,688 seats, award 4 marks. If answer given is 50,688 award, 3 marks. (4) Quest ion Number Analyse t w o entrepreneurial motives Nguyen Thi Phuong Thao may have had when starting Vietjet Air. Answ er Mark Know ledge Up to 2 marks for defining entrepreneurial motive, e.g. • Factors that drive (1) a person to start a business (1) OR For giving two factors that may have motivated Nguyen e.g. • Financial gain (1) Personal success (1) Applicat ion Up to 2 marks for answers contextualised the factors, e.g. • Nguyen may have seen opportunities in the expanding airline market in Vietnam (1) • Nguyen grew up in a country with few successful and dominant females in business (1) Analysis Up to 2 marks for reasons/causes/consequences of the motives e.g. • Nguyen may have seen the increase in income/tourism as a means of building the business to increase her own wealth (1) • This may have led Nguyen to work long hours growing the business to prove her capabilities and success. (1) (6) Quest ion Number Vietjet Air faces increased competition from new airline businesses in Vietnam. Discuss how increased competition may affect airline passengers in Vietnam. Indicat ive cont ent Answers must be credited by using the level descriptors (below) in line with the general marking guidance. The indicative content below exemplifies some of the points that candidates may make but this does not imply that any of these must be included. Other relevant points must also be credit
